PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
Off Calendar Apr-30-2019 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Alexander Jones for Duncan Barr (Petitioner).

The Court noted that no objections have been filed and that no one is here to object. The Court granted the Petition for Probate of Will and for Letters Testamentary (subject to review of the order).

Judge: John K. Stewart. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: K. Chan. Not reported.

PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
Off Calendar Apr-08-2019 Continued To Apr-22-19 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
Off Calendar Mar-06-2019 Continued To Apr-08-19 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances: Elizabeth A. Oliver (Niece of Thurston Barnhill) appearing by CourtCall in pro per.

Ms. Oliver stated that she was calling to object to the petition, that they didn't even know that her Uncle passed away until 12 days ago, that her Brother and Cousin received some paperwork, that she didn't receive any paperwork, and that they found out that the will was changed five days before her Uncle's death. In addition, Ms. Oliver stated that they've had no time to get an attorney, that both her Brother and Cousin are having surgery, that she's been dealing with that, that she lives in Florida, and that both her Brother and Cousin live in Louisiana. Ms. Oliver requested a six week continuance. The Court inquired whether Ms. Oliver would be retaining an attorney. Ms. Oliver stated that she'd contact one attorney but hadn't received a response yet. The Court then continued the matter to April 8, 2019 for status on the filing of objections.

Judge: John K. Stewart. Clerk: Jacqueline Alameda. Bailiff: Deputy K. Chan. Matter not reported.
